resources.string: To detect your Bluetooth headphones and run in the background, this app needs two permissions.
Bluetooth Connect — to detect your headphones
Notifications — to show balance status while running
Permission denied. Tap \'Open settings\' to grant it manually.
Connect a Bluetooth device to see it here.
Balance service is not running. Tap to restart.
Bluetooth permission was removed. Tap to re-grant.
Audio Balance fixes left/right imbalance on Bluetooth headphones. When you connect a device, it automatically applies your saved balance and gain settings.

The balance slider shifts audio left or right (−100 to +100). The volume adjustment slider reduces the overall volume of a device in decibels (−12 dB to 0 dB). Both settings are saved per device and applied automatically on reconnect.
Yes. Audio Balance is MIT licensed.
No significant impact. The app runs a foreground service that reacts to Bluetooth events only. There is no continuous polling or background processing.
Android 8.0 or higher. Bluetooth A2DP headphones only. Wired headphones and internal speakers are not supported.
Why is another audio app overriding my settings?
Another equalizer, bass booster, or audio app may be taking control of audio processing. Close it and reconnect your headphones.
